capabilities.summary: Build TileLang kernels with @tilelang.jit, @T.prim_func tensor annotations, and T.Kernel launch scopes.
capabilities.when_to_use: Start every new operator here to decide lazy/eager style, output handling, grid dimensions, thread dimensions, and target.
common_pitfalls: T.Kernel requires a Builder inside @tilelang.jit/@T.prim_func.
out_idx is lazy-mode only.
Threads default to 128 for GPU unless is_cpu=True.

expected_user_device_input.optional_arch: CUDA sm_XX, AMD gfxXXX, LLVM mcpu, or TVM target tag

target_selection_order: Use exact model/arch when supplied, e.g. cuda -arch=sm_90 or hip -mcpu=gfx950.
If only vendor is supplied, choose documented base target: cuda, hip, metal, llvm/c, or webgpu.
If no device is supplied, keep target auto but avoid architecture-specific intrinsics in generated code.

nvidia_cuda.applicability: Primary GPU path for most examples and advanced tensor-core features.
optimization_notes: Use tensor-core GEMM for fp16/bf16/int8/int4 where the architecture supports it.
Ampere-class sm_80+ has MMA support in carver arch helpers.
Hopper sm_90 enables WGMMA/TMA-related paths; explicit WGMMA requires manual waits.
Blackwell sm100/sm100a paths may use TCGEN05/TMEM/2CTA features; require matching target and cluster settings.
Use T.Pipelined and shared-memory staging for GEMM/attention memory-compute overlap.
limits: Architecture-specific intrinsics can fail compilation if the target does not support them.
Runtime tensors must be on the same CUDA device as the compiled kernel.

amd_hip.applicability: ROCm/HIP target path for AMD GPUs; examples under examples/amd and CDNA architecture helpers indicate supported tuning context.
optimization_notes: Prefer HIP target and CDNA-aware tile sizes when running on AMD accelerators.
Account for LDS/shared memory capacity; gfx950 has a 160KB LDS override in arch helpers.
T.gemm exposes k_pack noted as ROCm-specific in gemm_op.py.
Avoid CUDA-only WGMMA/TCGEN/TMA assumptions unless a HIP lowering path is explicitly present.
limits: Some examples and advanced intrinsics are NVIDIA-specific.
Device model to exact gfx target should be supplied by user or runtime tooling.

cpu.applicability: CPU/LLVM/C path for validation, scalar codegen, or non-GPU execution.
optimization_notes: Use T.Kernel(..., is_cpu=True) for CPU kernel frames.
Do not rely on GPU thread bindings, shared memory semantics, cp.async, WGMMA, TMA, or tensor-core paths.
Useful for correctness/codegen checks; performance strategy differs from GPU tiling.
limits: Many GPU-optimized examples assume CUDA tensors and are not directly portable.
CPU arch helper intentionally stores minimal detail.

apple_metal_webgpu.applicability: Documented targets for Apple Metal and WebGPU/browser runtimes; suitable only for patterns avoiding CUDA/HIP-specific intrinsics unless tests show support.
optimization_notes: Favor generic T.Kernel/T.Parallel/T.copy style and avoid tensor-core-specific or CUDA barrier APIs.
Treat memory scope and vectorization assumptions as target-specific until validated.
limits: The current operator-development examples are much richer for CUDA/HIP than Metal/WebGPU.
Advanced GEMM/attention tensor-core patterns have low confidence on these targets.

limitations: Use user-provided vendor/model to refine target and available intrinsics before generating final operator code.
optimization_decision_points: Select @tilelang.jit(target=...) or tilelang.compile(target=...) from vendor/model.
Use target auto only for portable examples; use exact arch for tuned kernels.
CPU target should use is_cpu=True where examples indicate CPU kernel frames.

capabilities.summary: Move and stage tiles among global, shared, local, fragment, TMA, and async copy paths.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use when an operator needs global-to-shared staging, fragment stores, async cp.async, TMA, or shared transposes.
common_pitfalls: copy extents must be deducible.
T.async_copy has explicit synchronization responsibility.
T.tma_copy loads require barriers.
T.copy broadcasting support is intentionally limited.

limitations: Use user-provided vendor/model to refine target and available intrinsics before generating final operator code.
optimization_decision_points: CUDA: T.copy may lower to SIMT, ldmatrix, TMA, or cp.async depending on target and hints; T.async_copy requires explicit wait.
Hopper/Blackwell: TMA and proxy-fence behavior can be target-sensitive.
AMD HIP: shared memory is LDS; use mcpu-specific capacity such as gfx950 160KB when sizing tiles.
CPU/Metal/WebGPU: avoid CUDA-only async copy/barrier assumptions.

capabilities.summary: Express block tiling, parallel loops, unroll/vectorize, persistent loops, and layout-aware parallel mapping.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use after choosing operator family to map work to CTAs, threads, pipeline stages, and layout annotations.
common_pitfalls: Parallel loop layout must match nest depth.
num_stages=0 disables software pipeline.
Swizzle and occupancy annotations are hints, not correctness fixes.

limitations: Use user-provided vendor/model to refine target and available intrinsics before generating final operator code.
optimization_decision_points: Tune block sizes, threads, num_stages, and layout annotations per device shared memory/register limits.
CUDA SM80+ favors tensor-core tile shapes; Hopper/Blackwell may prefer WGMMA/TCGEN-aware scheduling.
AMD CDNA should account for wave size/LDS and HIP target mcpu.
CPU target should prefer simpler loop structures and is_cpu=True.

capabilities.summary: Overlap producer/consumer work with T.Pipelined and backend pipeline passes.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use in K/reduction loops where copies and compute can be overlapped.
common_pitfalls: Incorrect stage/order/sync annotations can break lowering.
num_stages should match buffering capacity and memory footprint.

CUDA cp.async auto-lowering is gated by pipelined loop context and pass config.
Explicit async copy is not portable to CPU/Metal/WebGPU without target validation.

capabilities.summary: Use high-level tile ops that lower to MMA/WGMMA/TCGEN05/MFMA/scalar instructions.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use for matrix multiply, attention QK/PV, convolution-as-GEMM, and sparse tensor-core work.
common_pitfalls: T.gemm requires 2D C and compatible M/K/N.
Explicit WGMMA/TCGEN05 paths fail if target or operand pattern is unsupported.
TCGEN05 2CTA requires cluster_dims.

optimization_decision_points: CUDA: T.gemm dispatches target-specific tensor-core paths; explicit WGMMA is Hopper-specific, TCGEN05 is Blackwell-specific.
AMD: use HIP/MFMA-capable paths where supported; k_pack is documented as ROCm-specific.
CPU: T.gemm can use scalar path in CPU tests, not tensor cores.
Metal/WebGPU: treat tensor-core style patterns as low-confidence unless tested.

capabilities.summary: Build reductions, softmax stats, norm, cumulative sum, and warp-level aggregation.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use when operator needs row/block reduction, online softmax, top-k support, or norm statistics.
common_pitfalls: Output shape must match reduce dim contract.
Only shared/fragment scopes are accepted by reduce macro branches.
NaN propagation is narrow and CUDA-specific.

optimization_decision_points: Reductions are broadly useful across devices, but warp-level reducers and NaN behavior may be backend-specific.
For attention/softmax on GPUs, choose block sizes that fit shared/fragment memory.
CPU reductions should avoid GPU-specific warp assumptions.

capabilities.summary: Dense, split-K, sparse, dequantized, grouped, FP8/int4, and persistent GEMM examples.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use for any matrix-multiply-like operator or epilogue fusion.
common_pitfalls: Metadata/layout constraints for sparse/dequantized variants.
Atomic split-K needs correct output accumulation.
FP8/int4 kernels need dtype-specific tolerance and layout.

optimization_decision_points: Use exact NVIDIA sm or AMD gfx target for GEMM tuning.
For NVIDIA Ampere/Ada/Hopper/Blackwell, choose dtype/intrinsic path based on tensor-core support.
For AMD CDNA, use HIP target and verify LDS footprint.
For CPU, use CPU GEMM usage pattern rather than GPU shared-memory-heavy examples.

capabilities.summary: Flash attention, GQA/MHA, decode, paged KV, MLA-style examples and benchmarks.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use for attention kernels with QK/PV matmul, online softmax, variable length, or KV-cache indexing.
common_pitfalls: Numerical update order matters.
Layouts differ between BSHD/BHSD/GQA/varlen examples.
Paged/decode metadata must be consistent.

optimization_decision_points: CUDA/Hopper attention may benefit from TMA/WGMMA/layout inference; Blackwell may use TCGEN-related kernels where examples/tests support it.
AMD attention examples exist but should use HIP target and avoid NVIDIA-only barriers.
CPU/Metal/WebGPU coverage is low for advanced attention; use generic patterns only after validation.

capabilities.summary: Convolution examples and autotune flows, generally GEMM-like after tiling/im2col-style staging.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use for conv2d-like operators when output tiles can map to matrix multiply or im2col staging.
common_pitfalls: Boundary masking, padding, dilation, and data layout must be handled explicitly.
Coverage appears example-based rather than deeply documented.

target_selection_order: Use exact model/arch when supplied, e.g. cuda -arch=sm_90 or hip -mcpu=gfx950.
If only vendor is supplied, choose documented base target: cuda, hip, metal, llvm/c, or webgpu.
If no device is supplied, keep target auto but avoid architecture-specific intrinsics in generated code.

nvidia_cuda.applicability: Primary GPU path for most examples and advanced tensor-core features.
optimization_notes: Use tensor-core GEMM for fp16/bf16/int8/int4 where the architecture supports it.
Ampere-class sm_80+ has MMA support in carver arch helpers.
Hopper sm_90 enables WGMMA/TMA-related paths; explicit WGMMA requires manual waits.
Blackwell sm100/sm100a paths may use TCGEN05/TMEM/2CTA features; require matching target and cluster settings.
Use T.Pipelined and shared-memory staging for GEMM/attention memory-compute overlap.
limits: Architecture-specific intrinsics can fail compilation if the target does not support them.
Runtime tensors must be on the same CUDA device as the compiled kernel.

amd_hip.applicability: ROCm/HIP target path for AMD GPUs; examples under examples/amd and CDNA architecture helpers indicate supported tuning context.
optimization_notes: Prefer HIP target and CDNA-aware tile sizes when running on AMD accelerators.
Account for LDS/shared memory capacity; gfx950 has a 160KB LDS override in arch helpers.
T.gemm exposes k_pack noted as ROCm-specific in gemm_op.py.
Avoid CUDA-only WGMMA/TCGEN/TMA assumptions unless a HIP lowering path is explicitly present.
limits: Some examples and advanced intrinsics are NVIDIA-specific.
Device model to exact gfx target should be supplied by user or runtime tooling.

cpu.applicability: CPU/LLVM/C path for validation, scalar codegen, or non-GPU execution.
optimization_notes: Use T.Kernel(..., is_cpu=True) for CPU kernel frames.
Do not rely on GPU thread bindings, shared memory semantics, cp.async, WGMMA, TMA, or tensor-core paths.
Useful for correctness/codegen checks; performance strategy differs from GPU tiling.
limits: Many GPU-optimized examples assume CUDA tensors and are not directly portable.
CPU arch helper intentionally stores minimal detail.

apple_metal_webgpu.applicability: Documented targets for Apple Metal and WebGPU/browser runtimes; suitable only for patterns avoiding CUDA/HIP-specific intrinsics unless tests show support.
optimization_notes: Favor generic T.Kernel/T.Parallel/T.copy style and avoid tensor-core-specific or CUDA barrier APIs.
Treat memory scope and vectorization assumptions as target-specific until validated.
limits: The current operator-development examples are much richer for CUDA/HIP than Metal/WebGPU.
Advanced GEMM/attention tensor-core patterns have low confidence on these targets.

limitations: Use user-provided vendor/model to refine target and available intrinsics before generating final operator code.
optimization_decision_points: Map convolution to GEMM-like tiling when GPU tensor-core path is available.
For CPU/Metal/WebGPU, avoid assuming im2col/T.gemm lowering performance until validated.

capabilities.summary: Elementwise, activation, normalization, epilogue fusion, casts, and per-token transforms.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use for lightweight standalone ops or GEMM/attention epilogues.
common_pitfalls: Guard out-of-bound lanes.
Direct assignment vs T.copy staging affects generated memory access.
Alias buffers may require annotate_restrict_buffers.

limitations: Use user-provided vendor/model to refine target and available intrinsics before generating final operator code.
optimization_decision_points: Most portable family: start with T.Kernel/T.Parallel and add device-specific vectorization/coalescing only after target selection.
Use annotate_restrict_buffers when aliasing may affect generated code.

capabilities.summary: Understand and debug TileLang lowering, codegen, pass config, and backend-specific constraints.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use only after pattern/API layers are insufficient or compiler behavior must be explained.
common_pitfalls: Many symbols are internal transform passes.
Static graph edges may be inferred from pass names/tests.
Backend availability and architecture influence behavior.

limitations: Use user-provided vendor/model to refine target and available intrinsics before generating final operator code.
optimization_decision_points: Use pass_configs based on target: disable WGMMA/TMA/vectorization only for diagnosed target issues.
Use generated source and transform tests to verify architecture-specific lowering.

capabilities.summary: Run correctness checks, benchmarks, host checks, profiler, and regression examples.
capabilities.when_to_use: Use before finalizing an operator implementation and when selecting benchmark/tolerance strategy.
common_pitfalls: Hardware-dependent tests may require CUDA/HIP/CPU targets.
FP16/FP8/int4 tolerances differ from fp32.
Profiler tensor supply must match dynamic symbols.

Use CUDA profiler backends only for CUDA.
For dynamic shapes or metadata inputs, provide input_tensors to profiler.

expected_user_device_input.optional_arch: CUDA sm_XX, AMD gfxXXX, LLVM mcpu, or TVM target tag

target_selection_order: Use exact model/arch when supplied, e.g. cuda -arch=sm_90 or hip -mcpu=gfx950.
If only vendor is supplied, choose documented base target: cuda, hip, metal, llvm/c, or webgpu.
If no device is supplied, keep target auto but avoid architecture-specific intrinsics in generated code.

device_profile_usage: When user supplies vendor/model, resolve target first, then use capability.device_adaptation to choose pattern/API/source fallback constraints.
